author: anon-13168fa2
content: Plan: Write 03-real-world-skills/03-project-architecture.md. Core problem: agents default to god files and will cram everything into one file unless directed otherwise. Chapter needs to teach: (1) why single-file projects break down (context window limits, readability, merge conflicts, testing), (2) the god file problem specific to agents, (3) how to think about modules/separation of concerns conceptually, (4) DRY as a principle and how to direct agents toward it, (5) how to give architectural direction in prompts, (6) Rust-specific module structure (mod.rs, lib.rs, crate organization), (7) when to split and when not to (don't over-engineer). Practical focus: what to say to your agent to get good architecture from the start.
